input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

<current_datetime>2026-04-08T00:58:35.198Z</current_datetime>

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
Here's how to make your page theme-aware:

## XAML — `AppThemeBinding`

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page responds to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

`AppThemeBinding` reacts automatically when the OS theme changes — no event wiring needed.

## C# alternative — `SetAppTheme`

The equivalent in code-behind or C# markup:

```csharp
var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

var button = new Button { Text = "Get Started" };
button.SetAppTheme(Button.BackgroundColorProperty, Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"), Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
button.SetAppTheme(Button.TextColorProperty, Colors.White, Colors.Black);

Content = new VerticalStackLayout
{
    Padding = 20, Spacing = 16,
    Children = { label, button }
};
// Set page background
this.SetAppTheme(BackgroundColorProperty, Colors.White, Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));
```

**Signature:** `SetAppTheme(BindableProperty, lightValue, darkValue)`

## Key points

- **No manual subscription** — both approaches listen to `Application.RequestedThemeChanged` internally.
- **Scalable approach** — for larger apps, define theme colors as resources in `App.xaml` using `AppThemeBinding` so you update values in one place instead of per-element.
- You can detect the current theme at runtime via `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` (returns `AppTheme.Light`, `Dark`, or `Unspecified`).
